message:
feat(ci): expand crate acceptance gate with broader test coverage

Extend the crate acceptance gate script and test harness to cover
additional validation scenarios for the CI pipeline.

- tools/ci: expand run_crate_acceptance_gate.py with new check paths
- tests/ci: add test_crate_acceptance_gate.sh test scenarios
- Update beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-g0xq", "title": "Clarify changelog snapshot as historical and refresh test count", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "docs", "created_at": "2026-03-28T05:36:01.386521917Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-28T05:36:51.674079193Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-28T05:36:51.673729661Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id":"bd-g9h5","title":"[Epic] RaptorQ Erasure Decode with Symbol Recovery","description":"Implement actual erasure recovery in asx_raptorq_decode(). Currently, the decode function at src/raptorq/raptorq.c:120-127 just memcpy's all received symbols without any recovery from lost symbols:

    /* Simple decode: copy source symbols directly (repair symbols at end are ignored).
     * Full recovery from symbol loss would require the repair XOR equations. */
    memcpy(dst, syms, copy_len);

The encode side (lines 82-94) already generates real XOR repair symbols using the pattern: repair[j] = XOR of source symbols where (i + j) % r == 0. The decoder must invert these equations.

## Background
The RaptorQ module (src/raptorq/raptorq.c) implements a simplified XOR-based erasure code (not RFC 6330 compliant). It provides encode/decode for the asx codec surface. The encoder works correctly — it generates k source symbols + r repair symbols. But the decoder ignores repair symbols entirely, meaning any symbol loss results in data corruption.

## Algorithm
The XOR peeling decoder:
1. Build an equation matrix: each repair symbol covers a known set of source symbols
2. Iterative peeling: find any equation with exactly 1 unknown source symbol, solve it (XOR repair with known sources)
3. Repeat until all source symbols are recovered or no single-unknown equations remain
4. If stuck: declare unrecoverable (ASX_E_RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED)

## Scope
Wave A. The encode is real; the decode must match.

## Deliverables
1. New asx_raptorq_decode_with_erasures() API taking symbol_present bitmap
2. XOR peeling solver implementation
3. Decodability rank check (fail early if too many losses)
4. Updated existing decode to fast-path when all symbols present
5. Unit tests: no-loss, single-loss, max-recoverable, unrecoverable
6. E2E erasure recovery test with detailed logging
7. Conformance test vectors with known erasure patterns

## Files
- include/asx/raptorq/raptorq.h (modify: add new decode API)
- src/raptorq/raptorq.c (modify: implement recovery)
- tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c (modify: add erasure tests)
- tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c (new: e2e erasure scenario)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:57:24.555639771Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946426545Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:43:00.946241835Z","close_reason":"All RaptorQ tasks complete: API (bd-yf9u), XOR peeling solver (bd-1fsi), rank check (bd-3nny), unit tests (bd-zt9e), E2E scenario (bd-rwax). The simplified XOR erasure decoder recovers from single-symbol losses; multi-symbol recovery is limited by the peeling-only approach (documented in E2E test). Evidence: tests/unit/raptorq/test_raptorq.c, tests/e2e/e2e_raptorq_erasure.c. Artifact evidence: build/e2e/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-gdv6","title":"Harden crate acceptance gate JSON loading","description":"{"kind": "crate_acceptance_gate_summary", "run_id": "crate-acceptance-20260407T013625Z", "generated_at": "2026-04-07T01:36:25Z", "status": "fail", "required_doc_failures": [], "artifact_failures": ["e2e-manifest"], "family_failures": [], "report_file": "build/acceptance/crate_acceptance_crate-acceptance-20260407T013625Z.json", "summary_file": "tools/ci/artifacts/acceptance/crate_acceptance_crate-acceptance-20260407T013625Z.summary.json"} currently uses raw json.load() and can crash on malformed or empty JSON summary/manfiest files instead of failing with a clean gate status / usage error. Add defensive loading and regression coverage.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:25.293535003Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T01:36:32.093189052Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -161,6 +161,23 @@ test_gate_fails_when_family_docs_missing() {
     assert_contains "family listed in failures" "$output" 'bd-yx9r.19'
 }
 
+test_gate_fails_cleanly_on_invalid_e2e_manifest() {
+    echo "Test: gate fails cleanly on invalid e2e manifest JSON"
+    local repo="$TMPDIR_BASE/repo-invalid-e2e"
+    create_mock_repo "$repo"
+    printf '{invalid json\n' >"$repo/build/e2e-artifacts/e2e-pass/run_manifest.json"
+
+    local output rc=0
+    output=$(python3 "$GATE_SCRIPT" --repo-root "$repo" --run-id test-invalid-e2e 2>&1) || rc=$?
+
+    assert_eq "exit code" "1" "$rc"
+    assert_contains "summary lists e2e failure" "$output" '"artifact_failures": ["e2e-manifest"]'
+    assert_contains \
+        "report records invalid json" \
+        "$(cat "$repo/build/acceptance/crate_acceptance_test-invalid-e2e.json")" \
+        '"reason": "invalid-json"'
+}
+
 main() {
     echo "=========================================="
     echo "Crate Acceptance Gate — Unit Tests"
@@ -172,6 +189,8 @@ main() {
     echo ""
     test_gate_fails_when_family_docs_missing
     echo ""
+    test_gate_fails_cleanly_on_invalid_e2e_manifest
+    echo ""
 
     echo "=========================================="
     echo "Summary: PASS=$PASS  FAIL=$FAIL"

@@ -50,8 +49,20 @@ def latest_matching(root: Path, pattern: str) -> Path | None:
 
 
 def load_json(path: Path) -> Any:
-    with path.open("r", encoding="utf-8") as handle:
-        return json.load(handle)
+    try:
+        text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
+    except OSError as exc:
+        raise ValueError(f"{path}: unable to read JSON ({exc})") from exc
+
+    try:
+        decoder = json.JSONDecoder()
+        data = decoder.decode(text)
+    except json.JSONDecodeError as exc:
+        raise ValueError(
+            f"{path}: invalid JSON at line {exc.lineno} column {exc.colno}: {exc.msg}"
+        ) from exc
+
+    return data
 
 
 def text_contains(path: Path, needle: str) -> bool:
@@ -243,7 +254,7 @@ def latest_summary_for_mode(conformance_dir: Path, mode: str) -> Path | None:
     for path in sorted(conformance_dir.glob("*.summary.json"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime):
         try:
             data = load_json(path)
-        except Exception:
+        except ValueError:
             continue
         if data.get("mode") == mode:
             candidate = path
@@ -295,7 +306,17 @@ def check_summary(name: str, path: Path | None, pass_predicate: Any) -> dict[str

             return {"name": name, "status": "fail", "reason": "missing"}

@@ -332,16 +353,30 @@ def check_summary(name: str, path: Path | None, pass_predicate: Any) -> dict[str
         artifact_results.append({"name": "e2e-manifest", "status": "fail", "reason": "missing"})
         e2e_manifest = None
     else:
